[2018-06-11 05:26] mtanzer: @Phin @RiFT (#RIPBrowserers) I think the AI actually is deterministic, I believe Dave has said this before.  I believe it uses alpha beta search and then does playouts for its evaluation (instead of using a formula).  the playouts are actually done with a simpler weaker bot similar to expert bot iirc.  If you're seeing different results between web and desktop it's probably because desktop gets to run the native C++ code which is much faster while the web version has to run it in actionscript.  Although I'm not sure why the web version gives different turns sometimes even on the same machine.  Perhaps it can be explained by variance in how fast the code runs / how much the ai can compute within the timelimit, I imagine there are lots of factors that can affect this
[2018-06-11 05:27] mtanzer: oh and the order in which moves on your turn are played are certainly irrelevant, the AI just looks at the current board state to decide its move, it doesn't care what you did on your turn to get there
